titleStmt.title: Letter from Michael O'Connor to Matthew Nathan, 25 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Michael O'Connor

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Michael O'Connor (b.1876) to Matthew Nathan (1862-1939). Nathan was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.
This letter regards a proposed amendment regarding the Tralee and Dingle Railway (committee management) order 1896. In the letter O'Connor states the proposal was passed unanimously by the Tralee Urban District Council.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5613/7607

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Tralee Urban District Coucil.
Urban Council Chambers
Tralee. 25th April, 1916. Sir, I am directed by the Tralee Urban District Council to
inform you that, at their meeting held on the 7th.instant, they
had under consideration of the Draft Order proposed to be made
amending the Tralee and Dingle Railway (Committee of Management)
Order, 1896, which was transmitted with your letter of the 6th.
instant, and to state that the Amending Order as submitted was
unanimously approved of by the Council. I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant, M.O'Connor
Clerk of Council. The Under-Secretary,
Dublin Castle.
